purpose: Semantic project profiles for portfolio-aware recruiter and RAG retrieval.
notes: These profiles are derived from the current portfolio project pages and known repo descriptions.
They are intended to support intent classification, query rewriting, summary-first retrieval, and recruiter-style answer composition.
Confidence indicates how directly the profile is grounded in current on-site content.

profiles.title: Computer Vision Mini Conveyor System
profiles.summary: A custom hardware and edge-AI inspection system that automates SIM card staging, imaging, OCR workflow routing, and analytics from device capture through cloud validation.
profiles.business_problem: Manual SIM card processing was slow, expensive, and error-prone, limiting throughput and compromising data quality.
user_or_business_outcome: Cuts processing time from minutes per card to seconds.
Scales daily capacity from hundreds to more than ten thousand cards.
Reduces labor cost dramatically while improving accuracy.
strongest_engineering_signals: End-to-end ownership across hardware, edge compute, cloud integration, and analytics
Custom hardware design when off-the-shelf tooling is not enough
IoT telemetry and event-driven workflow automation
Practical computer vision and hybrid edge/cloud inference
capabilities: GPIO, stepper, servo, and sensor control
AWS S3 and AWS IoT Core ingestion
notable_tradeoffs: Uses a confidence-based routing strategy to balance edge speed against cloud verification quality.
Combines physical-system complexity with cloud validation instead of assuming vision alone is enough.
recruiter_questions: How does this project prove he can collect telemetry from physical systems and turn it into business value?
What part of this build best shows custom hardware and edge-system capability?
How did he connect device capture, cloud workflows, and analytics into one pipeline?

profiles.title: Low Code Economics
profiles.summary: A custom enterprise operations platform used to show when bespoke software beats low-code platforms on total cost, flexibility, offline support, and long-term control.
profiles.business_problem: Enterprise low-code platforms can accelerate delivery, but licensing, platform limits, and vendor lock-in can make them economically unattractive for core operational systems.
user_or_business_outcome: Demonstrates multi-year savings versus enterprise low-code licensing.
Supports more than one hundred concurrent users with custom workflows.
Keeps full ownership of data model, UX, and extensibility.
strongest_engineering_signals: Business-case thinking tied directly to software architecture
Full-stack enterprise application delivery
Role-based access, offline-first UX, and workflow design
Ability to translate operational requirements into product features
capabilities: Supabase auth, database, and realtime subscriptions
notable_tradeoffs: Slightly longer time to MVP than low-code in exchange for major multi-year cost savings and control.
Chooses custom engineering over platform convenience to avoid lock-in and licensing drag.
recruiter_questions: How does this project show that he can link technical delivery to business economics?
What does this build reveal about his full-stack and workflow-design strengths?
Why was a custom platform the better decision here than low-code?

profiles.title: ChefMind AI
profiles.summary: An AI recipe recommendation system that combines semantic embeddings, vector search, inventory awareness, and cross-platform product delivery to recommend meals users can actually make.
profiles.business_problem: Recipe apps often ignore what users already have on hand, leading to poor recommendations and unnecessary shopping friction.
user_or_business_outcome: Surfaces recipes matched to real ingredient availability, not just keyword overlap.
Keeps inventory, shopping, and recommendation logic in one flow.
Delivers the experience across web, mobile, CLI, and API surfaces.
strongest_engineering_signals: Applied AI beyond chat by using embeddings and vector retrieval in a product workflow
Cross-platform full-stack delivery
Hybrid ranking logic that mixes semantic similarity with practical availability
Product thinking around recommendation quality and user utility
capabilities: React Native and React web delivery
notable_tradeoffs: Uses a hybrid ranking model so semantic fit does not overpower ingredient availability.
Focuses on useful retrieval and ranking rather than treating AI as a novelty feature.
recruiter_questions: How does this project show practical AI product thinking instead of generic chatbot work?
What does the ranking approach reveal about his judgment in recommendation-system design?
How did he combine AI retrieval, backend logic, and product UX in one build?

profiles.title: AWS RAG - Portfolio Assistant
profiles.summary: A recruiter-facing retrieval-augmented chat assistant that answers questions about public GitHub projects with clickable citations grounded in repository content.
profiles.business_problem: Recruiters can open repositories, but they still struggle to quickly understand what the engineer built, how the system works, and why the work matters.
user_or_business_outcome: Turns public repositories into a grounded conversational portfolio interface.
Returns clickable citations so answers stay explainable and verifiable.
Makes technical work more accessible to recruiters without losing source fidelity.
strongest_engineering_signals: RAG system design with grounding and citation discipline
AWS-native cloud architecture across retrieval, storage, generation, and API delivery
Embeddable frontend product design
Strong full-stack ownership from data preparation to chat widget UX
notable_tradeoffs: Uses buffered responses and a typing indicator in V1 instead of pretending to stream over infrastructure that does not support it well.
Preserves github_url metadata throughout ingestion so trust and explainability survive retrieval.
recruiter_questions: How does this project show maturity in RAG architecture rather than simple LLM API usage?
What AWS trade-offs and retrieval decisions stand out in this implementation?
How did he make the assistant useful to recruiters while keeping it grounded in evidence?

profiles.title: ISP Uptime Monitor
profiles.summary: A business-enablement reporting system that captures network uptime data, centralizes it in Airtable, visualizes it in a dashboard, and generates stakeholder PowerPoint reports in one click.
profiles.business_problem: Weekly ISP uptime reporting was manual, time-consuming, and error-prone, taking operations time away from actual service delivery.
user_or_business_outcome: Reduces repetitive reporting admin work.
Improves reporting consistency and data accuracy.
Generates the stakeholder presentation directly from the browser.
strongest_engineering_signals: Business enablement through workflow automation
Internal tool design tied to real operational reporting cycles
Full workflow ownership from data capture to executive output
Practical serverless delivery with minimal infrastructure
notable_tradeoffs: Uses Airtable as a pragmatic operational store instead of building a heavier custom backend.
Keeps the final reporting artifact in-browser to remove the last manual formatting step.
recruiter_questions: What operational bottleneck did this project remove, and why does that matter to the business?
How does this work show business-enablement thinking rather than just dashboard development?
What made this system accurate, repeatable, and realistic for internal teams to use?

profiles.title: MetalSniper - Quantitative Trading System
profiles.summary: A hybrid Python and C++ quantitative trading platform that separates research, inference, execution, and risk across RL-driven directional signals and statistical arbitrage workflows.
profiles.business_problem: Many trading prototypes collapse research, execution, and risk into one fragile runtime, making the system hard to validate, unsafe to operate, and difficult to extend.
user_or_business_outcome: Creates a clearer architecture for quantitative research, execution, and monitoring.
Supports both directional ML trading and market-neutral stat arb inside one coordinated platform.
Improves operational discipline through watchdog startup, telemetry, and independent risk controls.
strongest_engineering_signals: Quantitative systems thinking rather than single-script automation
Machine learning integrated with explicit statistical and risk logic
Python/C++ separation for execution resilience and latency awareness
Research discipline through shadow journaling, postmortems, and skepticism about overfitting
capabilities: Stat arb logic with hedge ratios, z-scores, and half-life
Independent risk controls and watchdog runtime management
notable_tradeoffs: Splits Python intelligence from C++ execution to avoid coupling slower AI workloads to broker responsiveness.
Emphasizes runtime controls, validation, and reviewability rather than making the project depend on vanity PnL claims.
recruiter_questions: What makes this a quantitative systems project rather than just a trading bot?
How did he separate research, inference, execution, and risk in a technically credible way?
What machine learning and statistical arbitrage strengths are most visible here?

profiles.title: 4D Website
profiles.summary: A portfolio website that presents project case studies, recruiter-oriented positioning, and an embedded RAG assistant to help visitors navigate the engineer's strongest work.
profiles.business_problem: A normal portfolio shows projects but does not actively help a recruiter understand strengths, compare work, or navigate toward the most relevant evidence.
user_or_business_outcome: Packages technical projects into recruiter-readable case studies.
Highlights strengths across IoT, custom hardware, AI, cloud, and business systems.
Uses an embedded assistant to guide visitors toward the most relevant project evidence.
strongest_engineering_signals: Product thinking about personal branding and technical storytelling
Frontend delivery with structured case-study UX
Ability to turn a portfolio into an interactive technical product
Clear positioning across multiple engineering domains
notable_tradeoffs: Optimizes for clarity, positioning, and guided exploration instead of acting as a plain project gallery.
Uses project case studies plus interactive chat to make technical work more navigable to non-specialist readers.
recruiter_questions: How does the portfolio itself show product thinking and technical communication skill?
What does this site reveal about how he positions multi-domain engineering work?
How does the assistant improve recruiter navigation compared with a static portfolio?
